name: Cozumel
summary: Cozumel is one of Mexico's safest tourist destinations, featuring a low crime index of 34.22 and robust safety infrastructure. The island's tourism-driven economy ensures strong police presence in visitor areas, making it particularly safe for cruise passengers, divers, and families.
quickVerdict: Cozumel stands out as an exceptionally safe Mexican destination with low violent crime rates and excellent tourist infrastructure. While petty theft and tourist-targeted scams occur, particularly around ferry terminals and shopping areas, serious crimes are rare. The island's isolated location, strong police presence, and tourism-focused economy create a secure environment. Visitors should exercise normal travel precautions, avoid walking alone at night in poorly lit areas, and be cautious with valuables in crowded spaces.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown San Miguel
neighborhoods.description: Main tourist hub with strong police presence, shops, restaurants, and ferry terminals. Generally safe during day and early evening but avoid late night walking.

neighborhoods.name: Hotel Zone South
neighborhoods.description: Upscale resort area along the west coast featuring luxury hotels, protected beaches, and excellent security. Very safe for tourists day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Cruise Port Areas
neighborhoods.description: Well-monitored port zones including Puerta Maya and International Pier with heavy security presence. Designed specifically for tourist safety.

neighborhoods.name: North Hotel Zone
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential and tourist area with good safety record. Popular for vacation rentals but requires normal urban precautions after dark.

neighborhoods.name: East Coast Beaches
neighborhoods.description: Remote beaches with strong ocean currents and limited emergency services. Beautiful but isolated areas requiring extra caution, especially when alone.

neighborhoods.name: Interior/Jungle Areas
neighborhoods.description: Undeveloped areas away from tourist zones with limited police presence and poor road conditions. Avoid traveling alone, especially at night.

scams.name: Free Tequila Tour Scam
scams.description: Vendors offer free tequila tastings that lead to high-pressure sales of overpriced, low-quality alcohol.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book tours through licensed operators. Be wary of street vendors offering free tastings or tours.

scams.name: Fake Cuban Cigars
scams.description: Street vendors sell counterfeit Cuban cigars at touristy areas, claiming authenticity at bargain prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Purchase cigars only from reputable shops with authenticity certificates. Avoid street vendors making bold claims.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unlicensed taxis or drivers without meters charge inflated rates, especially targeting tourists from cruise ships.
scams.howToAvoid: Always agree on the fare before getting in. Use only licensed taxis from official stands or have your hotel call one.

scams.name: Timeshare Presentations
scams.description: Aggressive sales tactics promising gifts or discounted excursions in exchange for attending lengthy timeshare presentations.
scams.howToAvoid: Decline all timeshare offers. Book excursions through reputable operators only, not street vendors offering deals.

soloFemale.overview: Cozumel is considered very safe for solo female travelers, with many women living on the island independently reporting feeling secure. The island's laid-back atmosphere, strong community feel, and tourism infrastructure make it welcoming for women traveling alone. Most solo female visitors experience no safety issues when following basic precautions.
tips: Avoid walking alone at night in poorly lit or less populated areas, especially away from the main tourist zones
Don't leave drinks unattended and avoid excessive alcohol consumption when alone in bars or restaurants
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in tourist areas and use licensed transportation services
Join group tours or activities to enhance safety and meet other travelers

nightSafety.overview: Cozumel is generally safe at night when staying in well-lit tourist areas with active foot traffic. Most evening activities center around restaurants, beach clubs, and music venues rather than late-night street activity. Licensed taxis are widely available and commonly used after dark, providing safe transportation.
tips: Stick to well-lit areas with other people around, particularly downtown San Miguel and hotel zones
Use licensed taxis for transportation after dark rather than walking long distances
Avoid empty or isolated areas, especially residential neighborhoods away from tourist zones
Plan evening activities around specific destinations like restaurants or beachfront venues

transport.metro: No metro system exists on the island.
transport.rideshare: Traditional rideshare apps like Uber are not available on Cozumel due to local taxi regulations.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are the primary form of transportation and are generally safe. Always confirm prices upfront as most don't use meters. Use official taxi stands or have hotels call taxis to avoid overcharging.
transport.tips: Renting scooters is popular but risky due to high accident rates among tourists. Cars can be rented from major companies for safer island exploration.

customs: Greet each person individually when entering a room rather than giving a general hello to the group
Use formal titles and 'usted' form when addressing strangers, older people, or in formal situations
Tipping 10-15% at restaurants is customary for good service, with 10% sufficient at casual establishments

health.overview: Cozumel has adequate healthcare facilities including hospitals with 24-hour emergency rooms and multiple pharmacies. Private medical care is available and many facilities cater to medical tourists. Travel insurance is recommended for comprehensive coverage.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink. Use bottled or purified water for drinking and consider using it for brushing teeth. Even locals typically use bottled water for consumption.
health.vaccinations: CDC recommends being up-to-date on routine vaccinations plus Hepatitis A. Consider Hepatitis B for longer stays or potential exposure to bodily fluids. Typhoid vaccination may be recommended depending on activities.
health.altitude: No altitude concerns as Cozumel is at sea level. Main health risks include sun exposure, mosquito-borne illnesses like dengue and Zika, and traveler's diarrhea from contaminated food or water.

faq.a: Yes, Cozumel is one of Mexico's safest tourist destinations with a crime index of 34.22, strong police presence in tourist areas, and infrastructure designed for visitor safety. Violent crime against tourists is rare.

Cozumel is generally safe at night in well-lit tourist areas with active foot traffic. Use licensed taxis for transportation and avoid walking alone in poorly lit or residential areas away from tourist zones.

faq.q: Is Cozumel safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, many solo female travelers visit Cozumel safely. The island has a laid-back atmosphere with strong community feel. Follow standard precautions like avoiding isolated areas at night and not leaving drinks unattended.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Cozumel?
faq.a: Avoid remote east coast beaches due to dangerous currents and limited services, interior jungle areas away from tourist zones, and walking alone at night in residential neighborhoods. Stick to established tourist areas.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Cozumel?
faq.a: Licensed taxis are safe and the main form of public transport. Always confirm prices upfront and use official taxi stands. Rideshare apps aren't available, but hotel staff can call reputable taxi services.
